title: How to send an email
description: Learn to write and send your first email to family or friends.

steps.title: Open your email app
steps.description: Find the Gmail or Mail app on your phone or tablet. It looks like an envelope. Tap it once to open it.

steps.title: Start a new email
steps.description: Look for a pencil icon or a button that says 'Compose' or 'New Message'. Tap it. This opens a blank email for you to write.

steps.title: Enter the person's email address
steps.description: Tap the 'To:' field at the top. Type the email address of the person you want to write to, for example: john@gmail.com. Make sure there are no spaces.

steps.title: Write a subject
steps.description: Tap the 'Subject' field and type a short title for your email, like 'Hello from me' or 'Checking in'. This tells them what your email is about.

steps.title: Write your message
steps.description: Tap the large empty area and type your message. Write whatever you want to say, just like writing a letter. Take your time — no rush!

steps.title: Send your email
steps.description: When you are happy with your message, look for a 'Send' button or a paper airplane icon. Tap it once. Your email is sent! It usually arrives in seconds.

title: How to make a video call
description: See and talk to your family face-to-face using FaceTime, WhatsApp, or Zoom.

steps.title: Choose your app
steps.description: Popular apps for video calls are: FaceTime (iPhones only), WhatsApp (all phones), Zoom (for group calls). Make sure the app is installed. Ask a family member to help you install it if needed.

steps.title: Open WhatsApp (most common)
steps.description: Find the WhatsApp icon — it is green with a white phone. Tap it to open the app.

steps.title: Find the person
steps.description: Tap the name of the person you want to call. They must already be in your contacts and also have WhatsApp on their phone.

steps.title: Start the video call
steps.description: On their contact page, look for a video camera icon at the top right corner. Tap it. WhatsApp will start calling them.

steps.title: Wait for them to answer
steps.description: The phone will ring until they pick up. When they answer, you will see their face on your screen and they will see yours. Make sure you are in a well-lit room.

steps.title: End the call
steps.description: When you are done talking, tap the red phone icon to hang up. You can also tap the screen to see the buttons if they disappear.

title: How to connect to Wi-Fi
description: Connect your phone or tablet to the internet at home or at a café.

steps.title: Open Settings
steps.description: Find the Settings app on your phone — it usually looks like a gear icon. Tap it to open.

steps.title: Find Wi-Fi settings
steps.description: In Settings, look for 'Wi-Fi' or 'Wireless & Networks'. Tap it. You will see a list of available Wi-Fi networks nearby.

steps.title: Turn Wi-Fi on
steps.description: If Wi-Fi is off, you will see a switch or toggle. Tap it to turn it on (it should turn blue or green).

steps.title: Choose your network
steps.description: You will see a list of network names. Find your home network name (your internet provider or the name you set). Tap it.

steps.title: Enter the password
steps.description: Type your Wi-Fi password carefully. The password is usually on a sticker on your Wi-Fi router box at home. Tap 'Connect' or 'Join' when done.

steps.title: You are connected!
steps.description: If the password is correct, you will see a tick or the Wi-Fi symbol at the top of your screen. You are now connected to the internet!

title: How to share a photo
description: Send your favourite photos to family via WhatsApp or email.

steps.title: Open your Photos app
steps.description: Find the Photos or Gallery app on your phone. Tap it. You will see all your photos.

steps.title: Find the photo you want to share
steps.description: Scroll through your photos to find the one you want to send. Tap it once to open it fully.

steps.title: Tap the Share button
steps.description: Look for a share icon — on iPhones it looks like a box with an arrow pointing up. On Android it looks like three dots connected by lines. Tap it.

steps.title: Choose where to send it
steps.description: A menu will appear showing apps like WhatsApp, Gmail, Messages. Tap the one you want to use to send the photo.

steps.title: Choose who to send it to
steps.description: Select the person from your contacts. Add a short message if you like, then tap Send.

steps.title: Photo sent!
steps.description: Your photo is now on its way! It usually arrives within a few seconds if you have a good Wi-Fi connection.

title: How to use ChatGPT or Claude
description: Ask AI assistants questions, get help writing, and learn anything you want.

steps.title: What is an AI assistant?
steps.description: ChatGPT and Claude are like very knowledgeable helpers you can chat with. You can ask them anything — from recipes to medical questions to help writing a letter. They are available 24 hours a day.

steps.title: Open the website
steps.description: On your phone or computer, open your browser (Chrome or Safari). Type claude.ai or chat.openai.com in the address bar and press Go. You can also search 'Claude AI' or 'ChatGPT' in Google.

steps.title: Create a free account (one time only)
steps.description: The first time, you will need to create a free account using your email address. Tap 'Sign Up', enter your email and create a password. You only need to do this once.

steps.title: Type your question
steps.description: You will see a text box at the bottom of the screen. Tap it and type your question in plain, everyday language. For example: 'How do I remove a stain from a white shirt?' or 'Write a birthday message for my grandson.'

steps.title: Read the answer
steps.description: Press Enter or tap the send button. The AI will type out an answer in a few seconds. Read it at your own pace — there is no hurry.

steps.title: Ask follow-up questions
steps.description: If something is not clear, just ask again! Try: 'Can you explain that more simply?' or 'Can you give me an example?' The AI is always patient and never minds if you ask the same thing multiple times.

title: How to create a strong password
description: Protect your accounts with a password that is hard to guess but easy for you to remember.

steps.title: Why passwords matter
steps.description: A password is like the key to your front door. If someone guesses it, they can access your email, bank, or social media. A strong password keeps your accounts safe.

steps.title: What makes a strong password?
steps.description: A strong password is: at least 8 characters long, uses a mix of letters and numbers, and does not use obvious information like your name or birthday.

steps.title: Use a phrase you can remember
steps.description: Think of 3 words that mean something to you, like: BlueSky Morning Coffee. Then add a number and symbol: BlueSkyMorning42! This is very strong and easy to remember.

steps.title: Never share your password
steps.description: Never tell your password to anyone — not a bank, not Microsoft, not your internet provider. Real companies never need to ask for your password. If someone asks, it is a scam.

steps.title: Use different passwords for different accounts
steps.description: Use a different password for your email, your bank, and Facebook. This way, if one account is hacked, the others are still safe.

steps.title: Write it down safely
steps.description: It is fine to write your password in a notebook kept at home — but do not write 'Facebook password' next to it. Just write the website initial or a hint only you understand. Keep the notebook in a safe place.
